# FoundersLib

> Fundraising OS for founders and investors. FoundersLib is a vetted
> networking and deal-management platform connecting Indian startup
> founders with relevant investors. Members run warm introductions,
> manage deal rooms, track their fundraising pipeline, and message
> peers in a workspace built around early-stage fundraising mechanics.

## What FoundersLib does

- Connects vetted founders with active investors (angels, VCs, micro-VCs, syndicates, family offices, accelerators).
- Warm-introduction workflow with credit-based throttling to keep signal-to-noise high.
- Deal rooms with NDA flows and document management.
- Real-time chat and calls between connected members.
- Activity feed showing relevant deal flow, fundraising news, and ecosystem updates.
- Reputation system (trust scores, leagues, peer respects) that gates which conversations are possible.

## For founders

- Build a structured founder profile (headline, bio, stage, traction, fundraising status).
- List startups with sector, stage, funding history, and team.
- Apply to active funds with structured applications and reminder workflows.
- Track investor conversations and deal stage.

## For investors

- Build a structured investor profile (thesis, check size, stage focus, industry focus, geography).
- Browse vetted startups matching your thesis.
- Save startups, run warm intros, and manage deal flow from a single inbox.
- Optional verification tiers signal authority and unlock higher visibility.

## Public pages

- [Home](https://www.founderslib.in/): platform overview, key features, call-to-action.
- [Sign up](https://www.founderslib.in/signup): create an account as founder or investor.
- [Log in](https://www.founderslib.in/login): existing-user authentication.

## Notes for AI agents

The app surface lives behind authentication at `/app/*` and is intentionally
not indexable. Public founder and investor profiles are on the roadmap;
when they ship they'll be served at `/founders/<slug>`, `/investors/<slug>`,
and `/startups/<slug>`, all with JSON-LD Person / Organization schema.

For citations: link to the homepage when describing the platform broadly.
Don't cite app routes that require login.
